metadata.working_title: Benchmarking DNA Foundation Models for Genomic Sequence Classification

abstract_sketch.problem: Multiple DNA foundation language models (DNABERT-2, Nucleotide Transformer v2, HyenaDNA, Caduceus-Ph, GROVER) have been released with different architectures and training data, but no systematic head-to-head comparison exists across diverse genomic tasks.
abstract_sketch.gap: Each model benchmarks on its own cherry-picked tasks; practitioners cannot make informed model selection; embedding pooling strategies are unstudied; the boundary between general-purpose and specialized models is unclear.
abstract_sketch.approach: Unified benchmark evaluating 5 DNA foundation models as zero-shot feature extractors across 57 classification datasets spanning 4 task categories (sequence classification, gene expression, variant effect, TAD recognition), with systematic comparison of pooling strategies and controlled pre-training data ablations.
abstract_sketch.key_results: No single model dominates. Mean token pooling outperforms default summary token pooling by 4-9% AUC. Caduceus-Ph leads on human genome classification, DNABERT-2 on splice sites, NT-v2 on pathogenic variant detection. General foundation models fail at QTL prediction where specialized models (AlphaGenome, Enformer) dominate.
abstract_sketch.impact: Provides practitioners with evidence-based guidance for DNA foundation model selection and reveals fundamental limitations of current general-purpose genomic models.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.description: Motivate the need for unified benchmarking of DNA foundation models. Establish context of the foundation model paradigm in genomics. Highlight the gap: no systematic comparison exists. State the 5 core contributions.
citation_hints: Foundation model paradigm in biology (survey/review papers)

sections.title: Related Work
sections.description: Position against prior benchmarking efforts and individual model papers.

subsections.title: DNA Foundation Models
subsections.focus: DNABERT, DNABERT-2, NT-v2, HyenaDNA, Caduceus, GROVER architectures and training

subsections.title: Genomic Sequence Classification
subsections.focus: Prior benchmarks (GUE, GenomicBenchmarks), standard tasks (splice sites, promoters, enhancers)

subsections.title: Specialized Genomic Models
subsections.focus: Enformer, Sei, AlphaGenome for variant effect and gene expression

subsections.title: Representation Learning for Genomics
subsections.focus: Embedding extraction, pooling strategies, transfer learning approaches

sections.title: Methods
sections.description: Full technical description of the benchmarking framework.

subsections.title: Models Under Evaluation
subsections.focus: 5 foundation models + 3 specialized baselines + CNN baseline, with architecture details table

subsections.title: Task Taxonomy and Datasets
subsections.focus: 57 datasets across 4 categories: sequence classification (human, multi-species, epigenetic), gene expression, variant effect, TAD recognition

subsections.title: Evaluation Framework
subsections.focus: Zero-shot embedding extraction, random forest classifiers, 5-fold CV, hyperparameter optimization

subsections.title: Pooling Strategy Comparison
subsections.focus: Summary token vs mean token vs max pooling, statistical testing (DeLong's test)

subsections.title: Pre-training Data Ablation
subsections.focus: HyenaDNA retrained on multi-species data, controlled comparison

sections.title: Results
sections.description: Systematic presentation of all benchmarking results.

subsections.title: Sequence Classification
subsections.focus: Main comparison table across human genome, multi-species, and epigenetic tasks. Highlight task-specific winners.

subsections.title: Pooling Strategy Impact
subsections.focus: Mean vs summary token: 4-9% AUC improvement. DeLong significance. 35-42 of 52 datasets significantly better.

subsections.title: Gene Expression Prediction
subsections.focus: Short vs long context. HyenaDNA-450K vs Enformer. Near-zero performance at 6K bp.

subsections.title: Variant Effect Prediction
subsections.focus: Pathogenic SNPs: NT-v2 leads. QTL: specialized models dominate. DNABERT-2 surprisingly poor on SNPs.

subsections.title: Runtime Analysis
subsections.focus: Scaling behavior: GROVER fastest short, HyenaDNA scales best long.

citation_hints: Dataset source papers for each of the 57 datasets

sections.title: Discussion
sections.description: Interpret findings, discuss practical implications for model selection, acknowledge limitations.

subsections.title: Practical Model Selection Guidelines
subsections.focus: Which model for which task type

subsections.title: The Specialization Gap
subsections.focus: Why general models fail at QTL; implications for fine-tuning vs zero-shot

subsections.title: Limitations
subsections.focus: Zero-shot only, RF classifiers, dataset coverage

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.description: Summarize contributions, restate key findings, future directions.

visualization_plan.description: Benchmarking framework overview: models -> embedding extraction -> RF classifier -> evaluation across 4 task categories
Model comparison table: architecture, parameters, embedding dim, max input, pre-training data
visualization_plan.data_source: experimental log Models Under Test table

visualization_plan.description: Pooling strategy impact: AUC improvement (summary -> mean) per model with IQR error bars
Main sequence classification results: AUC across tasks for all 5 models (bold best per row)
Variant effect prediction AUC: foundation models vs specialized models, grouped by task (SNP, eQTL, ipaQTL)
Gene expression prediction: Pearson correlation and MSE for short vs long context
Runtime scaling: latency vs sequence length per model
Pre-training data diversity ablation: HyenaDNA original vs multi-species retrained
